   448→## 3.6 Material Management Tables
   449→
   450→```sql
   451→-- Inventory positions (snapshot from ERP/Maximo)
   452→CREATE TABLE inventory_positions (
   453→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   454→    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
   455→    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   456→    part_description TEXT,
   457→    
   458→    -- Quantities
   459→    quantity_on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   460→    quantity_reserved INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Soft + hard reservations
   461→    quantity_on_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   462→    quantity_available INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   463→        (quantity_on_hand - quantity_reserved) STORED,
   464→    
   465→    -- Replenishment
   466→    reorder_point INTEGER,
   467→    reorder_quantity INTEGER,
   468→    next_delivery_date DATE,
   469→    next_delivery_quantity INTEGER,
   470→    
   471→    -- Cost
   472→    unit_cost DECIMAL(12,2),
   473→    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
   474→    
   475→    -- Sync tracking
   476→    last_sync_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   477→    source_system VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'MAXIMO',
   478→    
   479→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   480→    
   481→    UNIQUE(depot_id, part_number)
   482→);
   483→
   484→CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_depot ON inventory_positions(depot_id);
   485→CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_part ON inventory_positions(part_number);
   486→CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_low_stock ON inventory_positions(depot_id, part_number) 
   487→    WHERE quantity_available <= reorder_point;
   488→
   489→-- Material reservations (link inventory to PM schedule)
   490→CREATE TABLE material_reservations (
   491→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   492→    pm_schedule_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_schedule(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   493→    inventory_position_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES inventory_positions(id),
   494→    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   495→    
   496→    quantity_reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
   497→    reservation_type reservation_type DEFAULT 'SOFT',
   498→    -- SOFT = tentative, can be released if PM moves
   499→    -- HARD = committed, parts allocated
   500→    
   501→    reserved_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   502→    reserved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   503→    expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- SOFT reservations auto-expire
   504→    
   505→    -- Fulfillment
   506→    fulfilled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   507→    fulfilled_quantity INTEGER,
   508→    
   509→    UNIQUE(pm_schedule_id, part_number)
   510→);
   511→
   512→CREATE INDEX idx_reservations_pm ON material_reservations(pm_schedule_id);
   513→CREATE INDEX idx_reservations_inventory ON material_reservations(inventory_position_id);
   514→CREATE INDEX idx_reservations_expiring ON material_reservations(expires_at) 
   515→    WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT' AND fulfilled_at IS NULL;
   516→```

   520→## 3.7 Man-hour Planning Tables
   521→
   522→```sql
   523→-- Man-hour capacity (resource availability by depot)
   524→CREATE TABLE manhour_capacity (
   525→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   526→    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
   527→    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
   528→    effective_to DATE,
   529→    
   530→    -- Base capacity per shift type
   531→    day_shift_hours DECIMAL(8,2) NOT NULL,
   532→    night_shift_hours DECIMAL(8,2) DEFAULT 0,
   533→    weekend_hours DECIMAL(8,2) DEFAULT 0,
   534→    
   535→    -- Staff counts
   536→    technicians_day INTEGER NOT NULL,
   537→    technicians_night INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   538→    specialists INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   539→    
   540→    -- Constraints
   541→    max_overtime_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 20.0,
   542→    min_utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 70.0,
   543→    target_utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 85.0,
   544→    
   545→    notes TEXT,
   546→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   547→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   548→    
   549→    UNIQUE(depot_id, effective_from)
   550→);
   551→
   552→-- Man-hour projections (generated by leveling engine)
   553→CREATE TABLE manhour_projections (
   554→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   555→    projection_run_id UUID NOT NULL,
   556→    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
   557→    
   558→    period_start DATE NOT NULL,
   559→    period_end DATE NOT NULL,
   560→    granularity VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,  -- 'WEEKLY', 'MONTHLY'
   561→    
   562→    -- Required hours breakdown
   563→    required_pm_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   564→    required_corrective_hours DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
   565→    required_overhaul_hours DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
   566→    required_total_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   567→    
   568→    -- Available hours
   569→    available_regular_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   570→    available_overtime_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   571→    available_total_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   572→    
   573→    -- Computed metrics
   574→    variance_hours DECIMAL(10,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   575→        (available_total_hours - required_total_hours) STORED,
   576→    utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   577→        (CASE WHEN available_total_hours > 0 
   578→              THEN (required_total_hours / available_total_hours * 100)
   579→              ELSE 0 END) STORED,
   580→    is_overloaded BOOLEAN GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   581→        (required_total_hours > available_total_hours) STORED,
   582→    is_underutilized BOOLEAN GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   583→        (required_total_hours < available_total_hours * 0.7) STORED,
   584→    
   585→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   586→);
   587→
   588→CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_proj_depot ON manhour_projections(depot_id, period_start);
   589→CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_proj_run ON manhour_projections(projection_run_id);
   590→
   591→-- Man-hour adjustments (proposed changes to level workload)
   592→CREATE TABLE manhour_adjustments (
   593→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   594→    projection_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES manhour_projections(id),
   595→    
   596→    adjustment_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   597→    -- 'ADD_OVERTIME', 'DEFER_PM', 'ADVANCE_PM', 'CROSS_DEPOT', 'ADD_CONTRACTOR'
   598→    
   599→    source_period_start DATE,
   600→    source_period_end DATE,
   601→    target_period_start DATE,
   602→    target_period_end DATE,
   603→    hours_adjusted DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
   604→    
   605→    affected_pm_ids UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',
   606→    affected_train_ids UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',
   607→    
   608→    -- Impact assessment
   609→    cost_impact DECIMAL(12,2),
   610→    risk_impact VARCHAR(20),  -- 'NONE', 'LOW', 'MEDIUM', 'HIGH'
   611→    risk_description TEXT,
   612→    
   613→    -- Workflow
   614→    status adjustment_status DEFAULT 'PROPOSED',
   615→    proposed_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   616→    proposed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   617→    approved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   618→    approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   619→    rejection_reason TEXT,
   620→    
   621→    -- Explanation
   622→    reason TEXT NOT NULL,
   623→    
   624→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   625→);
   626→
   627→CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_adj_projection ON manhour_adjustments(projection_id);
   628→CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_adj_status ON manhour_adjustments(status) WHERE status = 'PROPOSED';
   629→```

   633→## 3.8 Baseline & Drift Tables
   634→
   635→```sql
   636→-- Plan baselines (immutable snapshots)
   637→CREATE TABLE plan_baselines (
   638→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   639→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   640→    description TEXT,
   641→    version VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,  -- e.g., 'v3.2'
   642→    
   643→    baseline_date DATE NOT NULL,   -- "As of" date
   644→    horizon_end DATE NOT NULL,     -- How far into future
   645→    
   646→    -- Workflow
   647→    status baseline_status DEFAULT 'DRAFT',
   648→    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
   649→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   650→    
   651→    published_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   652→    published_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   653→    
   654→    superseded_by UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
   655→    superseded_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   656→    
   657→    -- Metrics at baseline time
   658→    fleet_count INTEGER,
   659→    total_pm_scheduled INTEGER,
   660→    total_manhours_projected DECIMAL(12,2)
   661→);
   662→
   663→CREATE INDEX idx_baselines_status ON plan_baselines(status);
   664→CREATE INDEX idx_baselines_date ON plan_baselines(baseline_date DESC);
   665→
   666→-- Baseline PM schedule snapshot
   667→CREATE TABLE baseline_pm_schedule (
   668→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   669→    baseline_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_baselines(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   670→    
   671→    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   672→    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
   673→    projected_date DATE NOT NULL,
   674→    projected_mileage_km INTEGER,
   675→    
   676→    assigned_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
   677→    assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
   678→    
   679→    UNIQUE(baseline_id, train_id, pm_type_id, projected_date)
   680→);
   681→
   682→CREATE INDEX idx_baseline_pm_baseline ON baseline_pm_schedule(baseline_id);
   683→CREATE INDEX idx_baseline_pm_train ON baseline_pm_schedule(train_id, projected_date);
   684→
   685→-- Baseline mileage projections
   686→CREATE TABLE baseline_mileage_projection (
   687→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   688→    baseline_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_baselines(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   689→    
   690→    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   691→    projection_date DATE NOT NULL,
   692→    projected_cumulative_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   693→    daily_rate_km INTEGER,  -- Assumed daily accrual rate
   694→    
   695→    UNIQUE(baseline_id, train_id, projection_date)
   696→);
   697→
   698→CREATE INDEX idx_baseline_mileage ON baseline_mileage_projection(baseline_id, train_id);
   699→
   700→-- Drift analysis results (generated by drift monitor)
   701→CREATE TABLE drift_analysis (
   702→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   703→    baseline_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
   704→    analysis_date DATE NOT NULL,
   705→    
   706→    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
   707→    
   708→    -- Mileage drift
   709→    baseline_projected_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   710→    actual_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   711→    mileage_drift_km INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS (actual_km - baseline_projected_km) STORED,
   712→    mileage_drift_pct DECIMAL(5,2),
   713→    
   714→    -- Schedule drift (days)
   715→    avg_pm_drift_days DECIMAL(5,1),
   716→    max_pm_drift_days INTEGER,
   717→    
   718→    -- Threshold breach
   719→    requires_replan BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
   720→    replan_reason TEXT,
   721→    
   722→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   723→    
   724→    UNIQUE(baseline_id, train_id, analysis_date)
   725→);
   726→
   727→CREATE INDEX idx_drift_baseline ON drift_analysis(baseline_id, analysis_date);
   728→CREATE INDEX idx_drift_replan ON drift_analysis(requires_replan, analysis_date) 
   729→    WHERE requires_replan = TRUE;
   730→```

   734→## 3.9 Scenario Management Tables
   735→
   736→```sql
   737→-- Scenarios (what-if analysis containers)
   738→CREATE TABLE scenarios (
   739→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   740→    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   741→    description TEXT,
   742→    
   743→    -- Fork point
   744→    forked_from_baseline_id UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
   745→    forked_from_scenario_id UUID REFERENCES scenarios(id),
   746→    forked_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   747→    
   748→    -- Metadata
   749→    status scenario_status DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
   750→    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
   751→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   752→    
   753→    -- Expiration (scenarios auto-expire after 30 days of inactivity)
   754→    last_modified_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   755→    expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT (NOW() + INTERVAL '30 days'),
   756→    
   757→    -- Promotion
   758→    promoted_to_baseline_id UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
   759→    promoted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
   760→    promoted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
   761→);
   762→
   763→CREATE INDEX idx_scenarios_status ON scenarios(status);
   764→CREATE INDEX idx_scenarios_user ON scenarios(created_by, status);
   765→CREATE INDEX idx_scenarios_expiring ON scenarios(expires_at) WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';
   766→
   767→-- Scenario deltas (event-sourced changes)
   768→CREATE TABLE scenario_deltas (
   769→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   770→    scenario_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES scenarios(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   771→    sequence_num INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Order of application
   772→    
   773→    delta_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   774→    -- 'PM_MOVE', 'PM_ADD', 'PM_REMOVE', 'CAPACITY_CHANGE', 'TRAIN_STATUS_CHANGE'
   775→    
   776→    -- Reference to affected entity
   777→    entity_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,  -- 'pm_schedule', 'manhour_capacity', 'trains'
   778→    entity_id UUID NOT NULL,
   779→    
   780→    -- Change details (JSON)
   781→    old_values JSONB,
   782→    new_values JSONB,
   783→    
   784→    -- Explanation
   785→    change_reason TEXT,
   786→    
   787→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   788→    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
   789→    
   790→    UNIQUE(scenario_id, sequence_num)
   791→);
   792→
   793→CREATE INDEX idx_scenario_deltas_scenario ON scenario_deltas(scenario_id, sequence_num);
   794→
   795→-- Scenario comparison results (materialized for performance)
   796→CREATE TABLE scenario_comparisons (
   797→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   798→    scenario_a_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES scenarios(id),
   799→    scenario_b_id UUID REFERENCES scenarios(id),  -- NULL = compare to baseline
   800→    baseline_id UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
   801→    
   802→    comparison_date DATE NOT NULL,
   803→    
   804→    -- Summary metrics
   805→    pm_count_delta INTEGER,
   806→    total_hours_delta DECIMAL(10,2),
   807→    total_cost_delta DECIMAL(12,2),
   808→    overloaded_weeks_delta INTEGER,
   809→    
   810→    -- Detailed differences (JSON)
   811→    pm_differences JSONB,
   812→    manhour_differences JSONB,
   813→    
   814→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
   815→);
   816→```

   823→-- Labor rates
   824→CREATE TABLE labor_rates (
   825→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   826→    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),  -- NULL = global default
   827→    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
   828→    effective_to DATE,
   829→    
   830→    -- Rate types
   831→    regular_hourly_rate DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,

   833→    weekend_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 2.0,
   834→    holiday_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 2.5,
   835→    
   836→    -- Shop rate (fully loaded)
   837→    shop_rate_hourly DECIMAL(10,2),  -- Includes overhead, benefits
   838→    
   839→    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
   840→    
   841→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   842→

   844→);
   845→
   846→-- Material cost history
   847→CREATE TABLE material_costs (
   848→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   849→    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
   850→    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
   851→    effective_to DATE,
   852→    
   853→    unit_cost DECIMAL(12,2) NOT NULL,
   854→    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
   855→    supplier VARCHAR(100),
   856→    
   857→    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   858→    
   859→    UNIQUE(part_number, effective_from)
   860→);
   861→
   862→-- Cost baselines (for variance analysis)
   863→CREATE TABLE cost_baselines (
   864→    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
   865→    fiscal_year INTEGER NOT NULL,
   866→    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
   867→    
   868→    budget_labor_hours DECIMAL(12,2),
   869→    budget_labor_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
   870→    budget_material_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
   871→    budget_total DECIMAL(14,2),
   872→    
   873→    -- Actuals (updated monthly)
   874→    actual_labor_hours DECIMAL(12,2),
   875→    actual_labor_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
   876→    actual_material_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
   877→    actual_total DECIMAL(14,2),
   878→    
   879→    variance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
   880→        (CASE WHEN budget_total > 0 
   881→              THEN ((actual_total - budget_total) / budget_total * 100)
   882→              ELSE 0 END) STORED,
   883→    
   884→    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
   885→    
   886→    UNIQUE(fiscal_year, depot_id)
   887→);
   888→```

  1025→
  1026→## 3.13 Canvas-Optimized Views
  1027→
  1028→```sql
  1029→-- Strategic zoom level (yearly aggregates)
  1030→CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_strategic AS
  1031→SELECT 
  1032→    t.id as train_id,
  1033→    t.train_number,
  1034→    t.fleet_id,
  1035→    date_trunc('year', ps.target_date) as year,
  1036→    COUNT(*) as pm_count,
  1037→    SUM(pt.estimated_hours) as total_hours,
  1038→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ps.status = 'COMPLETED') as completed_count,
  1039→    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ps.is_locked) as locked_count,
  1040→    SUM(pt.estimated_hours) FILTER (WHERE ps.status = 'SCHEDULED') as pending_hours
  1041→FROM trains t
  1042→LEFT JOIN pm_schedule ps ON t.id = ps.train_id AND ps.scenario_id IS NULL
  1043→LEFT JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
  1044→WHERE t.active = TRUE
  1045→GROUP BY t.id, t.train_number, t.fleet_id, date_trunc('year', ps.target_date);
  1046→
  1047→CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_strategic_train ON v_canvas_strategic(train_id, year);
  1048→
  1049→-- Tactical zoom level (weekly aggregates with PM details)
  1050→CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_tactical AS
  1051→SELECT 
  1052→    t.id as train_id,
  1053→    t.train_number,
  1054→    date_trunc('week', ps.target_date) as week_start,
  1055→    ps.id as pm_id,
  1056→    ps.target_date,
  1057→    ps.status,
  1058→    ps.constraint_start,
  1059→    ps.constraint_end,
  1060→    ps.is_locked,
  1061→    pt.pm_code,
  1062→    pt.estimated_hours,
  1063→    pt.display_color,
  1064→    tr.track_number,
  1065→    d.code as depot_code
  1066→FROM trains t
  1067→JOIN pm_schedule ps ON t.id = ps.train_id AND ps.scenario_id IS NULL
  1068→JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
  1069→LEFT JOIN tracks tr ON ps.assigned_track_id = tr.id
  1070→LEFT JOIN depots d ON ps.assigned_depot_id = d.id
  1071→WHERE t.active = TRUE
  1072→  AND ps.status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS');
  1073→
  1074→CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_tactical_train ON v_canvas_tactical(train_id, week_start);
  1075→CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_tactical_date ON v_canvas_tactical(target_date);
  1076→
  1077→-- Man-hour canvas overlay
  1078→CREATE VIEW v_manhour_canvas AS
  1079→SELECT 
  1080→    mp.depot_id,
  1081→    d.code as depot_code,
  1082→    mp.period_start,
  1083→    mp.period_end,
  1084→    mp.required_total_hours,
  1085→    mp.available_total_hours,
  1086→    mp.variance_hours,
  1087→    mp.utilization_pct,
  1088→    mp.is_overloaded,
  1089→    mp.is_underutilized,
  1090→    COALESCE(adj.adjustment_count, 0) as pending_adjustments,
  1091→    COALESCE(adj.total_adjustment_hours, 0) as proposed_adjustment_hours
  1092→FROM manhour_projections mp
  1093→JOIN depots d ON mp.depot_id = d.id
  1094→LEFT JOIN (
  1095→    SELECT 
  1096→        projection_id,
  1097→        COUNT(*) as adjustment_count,
  1098→        SUM(hours_adjusted) as total_adjustment_hours
  1099→    FROM manhour_adjustments
  1100→    WHERE status = 'PROPOSED'
  1101→    GROUP BY projection_id
  1102→) adj ON mp.id = adj.projection_id
  1103→WHERE mp.projection_run_id = (
  1104→    SELECT projection_run_id 
  1105→    FROM manhour_projections 
  1106→    ORDER BY created_at DESC 
  1107→    LIMIT 1
  1108→);
  1109→
  1110→-- Drift overlay (comparison to baseline)
  1111→CREATE VIEW v_drift_canvas AS
  1112→SELECT 
  1113→    da.train_id,
  1114→    t.train_number,
  1115→    da.baseline_id,
  1116→    pb.name as baseline_name,
  1117→    da.analysis_date,
  1118→    da.mileage_drift_km,
  1119→    da.mileage_drift_pct,
  1120→    da.avg_pm_drift_days,
  1121→    da.requires_replan,
  1122→    da.replan_reason,
  1123→    -- Color coding for canvas
  1124→    CASE 
  1125→        WHEN da.requires_replan THEN 'RED'
  1126→        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 3 THEN 'YELLOW'
  1127→        ELSE 'GREEN'
  1128→    END as drift_severity
  1129→FROM drift_analysis da
  1130→JOIN trains t ON da.train_id = t.id
  1131→JOIN plan_baselines pb ON da.baseline_id = pb.id
  1132→WHERE pb.status = 'PUBLISHED'
  1133→  AND da.analysis_date = (
  1134→      SELECT MAX(analysis_date) 
  1135→      FROM drift_analysis 
  1136→      WHERE baseline_id = da.baseline_id
  1137→  );
  1138→
  1139→-- Refresh materialized views (scheduled job)
  1140→-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
  1141→-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
  1142→```
  1143→
  1144→---
  1145→
  1146→## 3.14 Database Maintenance
  1147→
  1148→```sql
  1149→-- Automatic refresh of materialized views
  1150→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION refresh_canvas_views()
  1151→RETURNS void AS $$
  1152→BEGIN
  1153→    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
  1154→    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
  1155→END;
  1156→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1157→
  1158→-- Schedule refresh every 15 minutes (use pg_cron or external scheduler)
  1159→-- SELECT cron.schedule('*/15 * * * *', 'SELECT refresh_canvas_views()');
  1160→
  1161→-- Cleanup expired scenarios
  1162→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION cleanup_expired_scenarios()
  1163→RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
  1164→DECLARE
  1165→    deleted_count INTEGER;
  1166→BEGIN
  1167→    UPDATE scenarios 
  1168→    SET status = 'EXPIRED'
  1169→    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' 
  1170→      AND expires_at < NOW();
  1171→    
  1172→    GET DIAGNOSTICS deleted_count = ROW_COUNT;
  1173→    RETURN deleted_count;
  1174→END;
  1175→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1176→
  1177→-- Release expired soft reservations
  1178→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION release_expired_reservations()
  1179→RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
  1180→DECLARE
  1181→    released_count INTEGER;
  1182→BEGIN
  1183→    -- Update inventory positions
  1184→    UPDATE inventory_positions ip
  1185→    SET quantity_reserved = quantity_reserved - expired.total_qty
  1186→    FROM (
  1187→        SELECT inventory_position_id, SUM(quantity_reserved) as total_qty
  1188→        FROM material_reservations
  1189→        WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
  1190→          AND expires_at < NOW()
  1191→          AND fulfilled_at IS NULL
  1192→        GROUP BY inventory_position_id
  1193→    ) expired
  1194→    WHERE ip.id = expired.inventory_position_id;
  1195→    
  1196→    -- Delete expired reservations
  1197→    DELETE FROM material_reservations
  1198→    WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
  1199→      AND expires_at < NOW()
  1200→      AND fulfilled_at IS NULL;
  1201→    
  1202→    GET DIAGNOSTICS released_count = ROW_COUNT;
  1203→    RETURN released_count;
  1204→END;
  1205→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1206→
  1207→-- Update train target mileage for fleet balancing
  1208→CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_fleet_mileage_targets()
  1209→RETURNS void AS $$
  1210→BEGIN
  1211→    UPDATE trains t
  1212→    SET target_mileage_km = fleet_avg.avg_mileage
  1213→    FROM (
  1214→        SELECT fleet_id, AVG(current_mileage_km)::INTEGER as avg_mileage
  1215→        FROM trains
  1216→        WHERE active = TRUE
  1217→        GROUP BY fleet_id
  1218→    ) fleet_avg
  1219→    WHERE t.fleet_id = fleet_avg.fleet_id
  1220→      AND t.active = TRUE;
  1221→END;
  1222→$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
  1223→```
  1224→

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 3: Database Schema

**Document Version:** 2.0  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT

---

## 3.1 Schema Overview

The database schema is organized into the following domains:

| Domain | Tables | Purpose |
|--------|--------|---------|
| Core Fleet | `fleets`, `trains`, `depots`, `routes` | Asset master data |
| Mileage Tracking | `mileage_readings`, `meter_events` | Time-series odometer data |
| Component Tracking | `train_components`, `component_mileage` | Component-level lifecycle |
| PM Management | `pm_types`, `pm_schedule`, `pm_material_requirements` | Maintenance planning |
| Material Management | `inventory_positions`, `material_reservations` | Parts availability |
| Man-hour Planning | `manhour_capacity`, `manhour_projections`, `manhour_adjustments` | Workforce balancing |
| Baseline & Drift | `plan_baselines`, `baseline_pm_schedule`, `baseline_mileage_projection` | Plan stability |
| Scenario Management | `scenarios`, `scenario_deltas` | What-if analysis |
| Costing | `labor_rates`, `material_costs`, `cost_baselines` | Financial tracking |
| Audit & History | `pm_schedule_history`, `audit_log` | Change tracking |
| Users & Auth | `users`, `roles`, `sessions` | Access control |

---

## 3.2 Core Fleet Tables

```sql
-- Enable required extensions
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS timescaledb;

-- Enumerated types
CREATE TYPE fitness_status AS ENUM ('FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED');
CREATE TYPE pm_status AS ENUM ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS', 'COMPLETED', 'CANCELLED', 'DEFERRED');
CREATE TYPE track_type AS ENUM ('LIGHT', 'HEAVY', 'OVERHAUL', 'STABLING');
CREATE TYPE shift_type AS ENUM ('DAY', 'NIGHT', 'WEEKEND', 'HOLIDAY');
CREATE TYPE adjustment_status AS ENUM ('PROPOSED', 'APPROVED', 'REJECTED', 'APPLIED');
CREATE TYPE baseline_status AS ENUM ('DRAFT', 'PUBLISHED', 'SUPERSEDED', 'ARCHIVED');
CREATE TYPE scenario_status AS ENUM ('ACTIVE', 'PROMOTED', 'DISCARDED', 'EXPIRED');
CREATE TYPE material_criticality AS ENUM ('BLOCKING', 'DEFERRABLE', 'CONSUMABLE');
CREATE TYPE reservation_type AS ENUM ('SOFT', 'HARD');

-- Depots
CREATE TABLE depots (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    code VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    location VARCHAR(200),
    timezone VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'Asia/Singapore',
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Fleets (train types/manufacturers)
CREATE TABLE fleets (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    code VARCHAR(10) NOT NULL UNIQUE,  -- 'KS', 'ALSTOM'
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    manufacturer VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    model VARCHAR(100),
    base_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    cars_per_train INTEGER DEFAULT 6,
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Trains
CREATE TABLE trains (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
    train_number VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    commissioning_date DATE,
    current_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    fitness_status fitness_status DEFAULT 'FIT',
    fitness_notes TEXT,
    current_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    home_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    
    -- For mileage balancing
    target_mileage_km INTEGER,  -- Calculated target for fleet balance
    mileage_variance_km INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (current_mileage_km - COALESCE(target_mileage_km, current_mileage_km)) STORED,
    
    -- Canvas display properties
    display_color VARCHAR(7),
    display_order INTEGER,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fleet ON trains(fleet_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fitness ON trains(fitness_status) WHERE active = TRUE;
CREATE INDEX idx_trains_depot ON trains(current_depot_id);

-- Routes
CREATE TABLE routes (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    route_code VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    route_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    line_id VARCHAR(20),
    expected_km_per_day INTEGER NOT NULL,
    required_trains INTEGER NOT NULL,
    
    -- Restrictions
    allowed_fleet_ids UUID[],
    requires_ato BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    min_cars INTEGER,
    max_cars INTEGER,
    
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Tracks (maintenance bays)
CREATE TABLE tracks (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
    track_number VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    track_type track_type NOT NULL,
    max_train_length_m DECIMAL(5,2),
    has_overhead_crane BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    has_pit_access BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(depot_id, track_number)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_tracks_depot_type ON tracks(depot_id, track_type) WHERE active = TRUE;
```

---

## 3.3 Mileage Tracking Tables

```sql
-- Meter events (odometer replacements, resets, swaps)
CREATE TABLE meter_events (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    event_date DATE NOT NULL,
    event_type VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,  -- 'REPLACEMENT', 'RESET', 'CALIBRATION', 'SWAP'
    
    old_meter_serial VARCHAR(50),
    new_meter_serial VARCHAR(50),
    old_meter_reading INTEGER,
    new_meter_reading INTEGER,
    adjustment_km INTEGER,  -- Cumulative mileage adjustment to apply
    
    reason TEXT,
    approved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_meter_events_train ON meter_events(train_id, event_date DESC);

-- Daily mileage readings (TimescaleDB hypertable)
CREATE TABLE mileage_readings (
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    reading_date DATE NOT NULL,
    
    -- Raw and adjusted values
    raw_reading INTEGER NOT NULL,        -- Odometer value from CSV
    adjusted_reading INTEGER NOT NULL,   -- After meter event adjustments
    daily_km INTEGER,                    -- Calculated: today - yesterday
    
    -- Validation
    validation_status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'PENDING',  -- 'PENDING', 'VALID', 'ANOMALY', 'CORRECTED'
    anomaly_type VARCHAR(30),            -- 'NEGATIVE', 'SPIKE', 'MISSING', 'DUPLICATE'
    anomaly_notes TEXT,
    
    -- Source tracking
    source_file VARCHAR(255),
    meter_event_id UUID REFERENCES meter_events(id),
    
    ingested_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    PRIMARY KEY (train_id, reading_date)
);

-- Convert to TimescaleDB hypertable
SELECT create_hypertable('mileage_readings', 'reading_date', 
    chunk_time_interval => INTERVAL '7 days',
    if_not_exists => TRUE
);

-- Compression policy (compress data older than 30 days)
SELECT add_compression_policy('mileage_readings', INTERVAL '30 days');

-- Retention policy (keep 13 years for contract + buffer)
SELECT add_retention_policy('mileage_readings', INTERVAL '13 years');

-- Indexes for common queries
CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_train_date ON mileage_readings (train_id, reading_date DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_validation ON mileage_readings (validation_status, reading_date DESC) 
    WHERE validation_status != 'VALID';

-- Continuous aggregate for dashboard queries
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW daily_fleet_mileage
WITH (timescaledb.continuous) AS
SELECT 
    time_bucket('1 day', reading_date) AS bucket,
    t.fleet_id,
    COUNT(*) as train_count,
    SUM(daily_km) as total_daily_km,
    AVG(daily_km) as avg_daily_km,
    MIN(daily_km) as min_daily_km,
    MAX(daily_km) as max_daily_km,
    STDDEV(daily_km) as stddev_daily_km
FROM mileage_readings mr
JOIN trains t ON mr.train_id = t.id
WHERE mr.validation_status = 'VALID'
GROUP BY bucket, t.fleet_id
WITH NO DATA;

SELECT add_continuous_aggregate_policy('daily_fleet_mileage',
    start_offset => INTERVAL '3 days',
    end_offset => INTERVAL '1 hour',
    schedule_interval => INTERVAL '1 hour'
);
```

---

## 3.4 Component Tracking Tables

```sql
-- Train components (bogies, motors, pantographs, etc.)
CREATE TABLE train_components (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    component_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,  -- 'BOGIE', 'TRACTION_MOTOR', 'PANTOGRAPH', 'BRAKE_UNIT'
    serial_number VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    manufacturer VARCHAR(100),
    model VARCHAR(100),
    
    -- Current installation
    current_train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
    current_position VARCHAR(50),  -- e.g., 'CAR_1_BOGIE_A', 'CAR_3_MOTOR_L'
    installed_date DATE,
    installed_mileage_km INTEGER,  -- Train mileage at installation
    
    -- Component lifecycle
    commissioning_date DATE,
    component_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Cumulative mileage on this component
    last_overhaul_date DATE,
    last_overhaul_mileage_km INTEGER,
    
    -- Status
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'IN_SERVICE',  -- 'IN_SERVICE', 'SPARE', 'IN_OVERHAUL', 'CONDEMNED'
    condition_score INTEGER,  -- 1-100 condition rating
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_components_train ON train_components(current_train_id) WHERE status = 'IN_SERVICE';
CREATE INDEX idx_components_type ON train_components(component_type, status);

-- Component installation history
CREATE TABLE component_installations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    component_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES train_components(id),
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    position VARCHAR(50),
    
    installed_date DATE NOT NULL,
    installed_train_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    installed_component_mileage_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    
    removed_date DATE,
    removed_train_mileage_km INTEGER,
    removed_component_mileage_km INTEGER,
    
    removal_reason VARCHAR(50),  -- 'SCHEDULED_OVERHAUL', 'FAILURE', 'SWAP', 'UPGRADE'
    work_order_id VARCHAR(50),   -- Link to Maximo
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_installations_component ON component_installations(component_id, installed_date DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_installations_train ON component_installations(train_id, installed_date DESC);

-- Component-based mileage adjustments
-- When a component is swapped, PM thresholds follow the component, not the train
CREATE TABLE component_pm_tracking (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    component_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES train_components(id),
    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
    
    last_pm_date DATE,
    last_pm_mileage_km INTEGER,
    next_pm_due_mileage_km INTEGER,
    next_pm_due_date DATE,
    
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(component_id, pm_type_id)
);
```

---

## 3.5 PM Management Tables

```sql
-- PM types (maintenance task definitions)
CREATE TABLE pm_types (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
    pm_code VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,      -- 'C1', 'C2', 'C3', 'C4', 'OVERHAUL'
    pm_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    version INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
    
    -- Interval thresholds (whichever comes first)
    interval_km INTEGER,               -- Mileage-based trigger
    interval_months INTEGER,           -- Time-based trigger (industry standard)
    
    -- PM Hierarchy: which lower-level PMs does this PM satisfy?
    -- When a C4 is completed, it satisfies C1/C2/C3 requirements
    -- This resets their mileage counters from the C4 completion point
    -- Example: C4 row has satisfies_codes = ['C1', 'C2', 'C3']
    satisfies_codes VARCHAR(20)[],
    
    -- Resource requirements
    estimated_hours DECIMAL(6,2) NOT NULL,
    track_type track_type NOT NULL,
    min_track_days INTEGER DEFAULT 1,  -- How many days track is occupied
    
    -- Compliance window (tolerance for scheduling)
    early_tolerance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 10.0,   -- Can do 10% early
    late_tolerance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 10.0,    -- Can do 10% late
    
    -- Certification requirements
    required_certifications VARCHAR(50)[],
    
    -- Canvas display
    display_color VARCHAR(7),
    display_priority INTEGER DEFAULT 50,
    
    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(fleet_id, pm_code, version)
);

-- Example PM hierarchy data:
-- INSERT INTO pm_types (fleet_id, pm_code, pm_name, interval_km, interval_months, satisfies_codes, ...)
-- KS Fleet:
--   C1: interval_km=12500, interval_months=6,  satisfies_codes=NULL
--   C2: interval_km=50000, interval_months=24, satisfies_codes=['C1']
--   C3: interval_km=100000, interval_months=48, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2']
--   C4: interval_km=200000, interval_months=96, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2','C3']
--   OH: interval_km=800000, interval_months=NULL, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2','C3','C4']

-- PM material requirements (Bill of Materials)
CREATE TABLE pm_material_requirements (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    part_description TEXT,
    
    quantity_per_pm INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    criticality material_criticality NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BLOCKING',
    -- BLOCKING = cannot start PM without part
    -- DEFERRABLE = can start, must have within X days
    -- CONSUMABLE = nice-to-have, can substitute
    
    lead_time_days INTEGER DEFAULT 14,
    substitute_parts VARCHAR(50)[],    -- Alternative part numbers
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(pm_type_id, part_number)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_pm_materials_type ON pm_material_requirements(pm_type_id);

-- PM schedule (planned and executed maintenance)
CREATE TABLE pm_schedule (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
    
    -- Scheduling
    target_date DATE NOT NULL,
    target_mileage_km INTEGER,
    status pm_status DEFAULT 'SCHEDULED',
    
    -- Compliance window (calculated from pm_types tolerances)
    constraint_start DATE NOT NULL,    -- Earliest allowed date
    constraint_end DATE NOT NULL,      -- Latest allowed date (statutory lock after this)
    
    -- Resource assignment
    assigned_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    assigned_track_id UUID REFERENCES tracks(id),
    assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
    assigned_shift shift_type,
    
    -- Execution
    actual_start_date DATE,
    actual_end_date DATE,
    actual_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    completed_mileage_km INTEGER,
    
    -- Work order integration
    maximo_wo_id VARCHAR(50),
    
    -- Locking
    is_locked BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,   -- Cannot be moved by optimizer
    locked_reason VARCHAR(100),
    locked_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    locked_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    
    -- Scenario support (NULL = production schedule)
    scenario_id UUID REFERENCES scenarios(id),
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_train ON pm_schedule(train_id, target_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_date ON pm_schedule(target_date, status);
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_depot ON pm_schedule(assigned_depot_id, target_date) 
    WHERE status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS');
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_scenario ON pm_schedule(scenario_id) WHERE scenario_id IS NOT NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_canvas ON pm_schedule(target_date, train_id)
    WHERE status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS') AND scenario_id IS NULL;
```

---

## 3.6 Material Management Tables

```sql
-- Inventory positions (snapshot from ERP/Maximo)
CREATE TABLE inventory_positions (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    part_description TEXT,
    
    -- Quantities
    quantity_on_hand INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    quantity_reserved INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Soft + hard reservations
    quantity_on_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    quantity_available INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (quantity_on_hand - quantity_reserved) STORED,
    
    -- Replenishment
    reorder_point INTEGER,
    reorder_quantity INTEGER,
    next_delivery_date DATE,
    next_delivery_quantity INTEGER,
    
    -- Cost
    unit_cost DECIMAL(12,2),
    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
    
    -- Sync tracking
    last_sync_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    source_system VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'MAXIMO',
    
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(depot_id, part_number)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_depot ON inventory_positions(depot_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_part ON inventory_positions(part_number);
CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_low_stock ON inventory_positions(depot_id, part_number) 
    WHERE quantity_available <= reorder_point;

-- Material reservations (link inventory to PM schedule)
CREATE TABLE material_reservations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    pm_schedule_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_schedule(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    inventory_position_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES inventory_positions(id),
    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    
    quantity_reserved INTEGER NOT NULL,
    reservation_type reservation_type DEFAULT 'SOFT',
    -- SOFT = tentative, can be released if PM moves
    -- HARD = committed, parts allocated
    
    reserved_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    reserved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ,  -- SOFT reservations auto-expire
    
    -- Fulfillment
    fulfilled_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    fulfilled_quantity INTEGER,
    
    UNIQUE(pm_schedule_id, part_number)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_reservations_pm ON material_reservations(pm_schedule_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_reservations_inventory ON material_reservations(inventory_position_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_reservations_expiring ON material_reservations(expires_at) 
    WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT' AND fulfilled_at IS NULL;
```

---

## 3.7 Man-hour Planning Tables

```sql
-- Man-hour capacity (resource availability by depot)
CREATE TABLE manhour_capacity (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
    effective_to DATE,
    
    -- Base capacity per shift type
    day_shift_hours DECIMAL(8,2) NOT NULL,
    night_shift_hours DECIMAL(8,2) DEFAULT 0,
    weekend_hours DECIMAL(8,2) DEFAULT 0,
    
    -- Staff counts
    technicians_day INTEGER NOT NULL,
    technicians_night INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    specialists INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    
    -- Constraints
    max_overtime_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 20.0,
    min_utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 70.0,
    target_utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 85.0,
    
    notes TEXT,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(depot_id, effective_from)
);

-- Man-hour projections (generated by leveling engine)
CREATE TABLE manhour_projections (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    projection_run_id UUID NOT NULL,
    depot_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES depots(id),
    
    period_start DATE NOT NULL,
    period_end DATE NOT NULL,
    granularity VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,  -- 'WEEKLY', 'MONTHLY'
    
    -- Required hours breakdown
    required_pm_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    required_corrective_hours DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
    required_overhaul_hours DECIMAL(10,2) DEFAULT 0,
    required_total_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    
    -- Available hours
    available_regular_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    available_overtime_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    available_total_hours DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    
    -- Computed metrics
    variance_hours DECIMAL(10,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (available_total_hours - required_total_hours) STORED,
    utilization_pct DECIMAL(5,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (CASE WHEN available_total_hours > 0 
              THEN (required_total_hours / available_total_hours * 100)
              ELSE 0 END) STORED,
    is_overloaded BOOLEAN GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (required_total_hours > available_total_hours) STORED,
    is_underutilized BOOLEAN GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (required_total_hours < available_total_hours * 0.7) STORED,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_proj_depot ON manhour_projections(depot_id, period_start);
CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_proj_run ON manhour_projections(projection_run_id);

-- Man-hour adjustments (proposed changes to level workload)
CREATE TABLE manhour_adjustments (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    projection_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES manhour_projections(id),
    
    adjustment_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    -- 'ADD_OVERTIME', 'DEFER_PM', 'ADVANCE_PM', 'CROSS_DEPOT', 'ADD_CONTRACTOR'
    
    source_period_start DATE,
    source_period_end DATE,
    target_period_start DATE,
    target_period_end DATE,
    hours_adjusted DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    
    affected_pm_ids UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',
    affected_train_ids UUID[] DEFAULT '{}',
    
    -- Impact assessment
    cost_impact DECIMAL(12,2),
    risk_impact VARCHAR(20),  -- 'NONE', 'LOW', 'MEDIUM', 'HIGH'
    risk_description TEXT,
    
    -- Workflow
    status adjustment_status DEFAULT 'PROPOSED',
    proposed_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    proposed_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    approved_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    rejection_reason TEXT,
    
    -- Explanation
    reason TEXT NOT NULL,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_adj_projection ON manhour_adjustments(projection_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_manhour_adj_status ON manhour_adjustments(status) WHERE status = 'PROPOSED';
```

## 3.8 Baseline & Drift Tables

```sql
-- Plan baselines (immutable snapshots)
CREATE TABLE plan_baselines (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    version VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,  -- e.g., 'v3.2'
    
    baseline_date DATE NOT NULL,   -- "As of" date
    horizon_end DATE NOT NULL,     -- How far into future
    
    -- Workflow
    status baseline_status DEFAULT 'DRAFT',
    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    published_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    published_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    
    superseded_by UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
    superseded_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    
    -- Metrics at baseline time
    fleet_count INTEGER,
    total_pm_scheduled INTEGER,
    total_manhours_projected DECIMAL(12,2)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_baselines_status ON plan_baselines(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_baselines_date ON plan_baselines(baseline_date DESC);

-- Baseline PM schedule snapshot
CREATE TABLE baseline_pm_schedule (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    baseline_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_baselines(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
    projected_date DATE NOT NULL,
    projected_mileage_km INTEGER,
    
    assigned_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
    
    UNIQUE(baseline_id, train_id, pm_type_id, projected_date)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_baseline_pm_baseline ON baseline_pm_schedule(baseline_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_baseline_pm_train ON baseline_pm_schedule(train_id, projected_date);

-- Baseline mileage projections
CREATE TABLE baseline_mileage_projection (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    baseline_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_baselines(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    projection_date DATE NOT NULL,
    projected_cumulative_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    daily_rate_km INTEGER,  -- Assumed daily accrual rate
    
    UNIQUE(baseline_id, train_id, projection_date)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_baseline_mileage ON baseline_mileage_projection(baseline_id, train_id);

-- Drift analysis results (generated by drift monitor)
CREATE TABLE drift_analysis (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    baseline_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
    analysis_date DATE NOT NULL,
    
    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
    
    -- Mileage drift
    baseline_projected_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    actual_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    mileage_drift_km INTEGER GENERATED ALWAYS AS (actual_km - baseline_projected_km) STORED,
    mileage_drift_pct DECIMAL(5,2),
    
    -- Schedule drift (days)
    avg_pm_drift_days DECIMAL(5,1),
    max_pm_drift_days INTEGER,
    
    -- Threshold breach
    requires_replan BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
    replan_reason TEXT,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(baseline_id, train_id, analysis_date)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_drift_baseline ON drift_analysis(baseline_id, analysis_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_drift_replan ON drift_analysis(requires_replan, analysis_date) 
    WHERE requires_replan = TRUE;
```

## 3.9 Scenario Management Tables

```sql
-- Scenarios (what-if analysis containers)
CREATE TABLE scenarios (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    description TEXT,
    
    -- Fork point
    forked_from_baseline_id UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
    forked_from_scenario_id UUID REFERENCES scenarios(id),
    forked_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    -- Metadata
    status scenario_status DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
    created_by UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    -- Expiration (scenarios auto-expire after 30 days of inactivity)
    last_modified_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT (NOW() + INTERVAL '30 days'),
    
    -- Promotion
    promoted_to_baseline_id UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
    promoted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
    promoted_by UUID REFERENCES users(id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_scenarios_status ON scenarios(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_scenarios_user ON scenarios(created_by, status);
CREATE INDEX idx_scenarios_expiring ON scenarios(expires_at) WHERE status = 'ACTIVE';

-- Scenario deltas (event-sourced changes)
CREATE TABLE scenario_deltas (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    scenario_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES scenarios(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    sequence_num INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Order of application
    
    delta_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    -- 'PM_MOVE', 'PM_ADD', 'PM_REMOVE', 'CAPACITY_CHANGE', 'TRAIN_STATUS_CHANGE'
    
    -- Reference to affected entity
    entity_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,  -- 'pm_schedule', 'manhour_capacity', 'trains'
    entity_id UUID NOT NULL,
    
    -- Change details (JSON)
    old_values JSONB,
    new_values JSONB,
    
    -- Explanation
    change_reason TEXT,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    created_by UUID REFERENCES users(id),
    
    UNIQUE(scenario_id, sequence_num)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_scenario_deltas_scenario ON scenario_deltas(scenario_id, sequence_num);

-- Scenario comparison results (materialized for performance)
CREATE TABLE scenario_comparisons (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    scenario_a_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES scenarios(id),
    scenario_b_id UUID REFERENCES scenarios(id),  -- NULL = compare to baseline
    baseline_id UUID REFERENCES plan_baselines(id),
    
    comparison_date DATE NOT NULL,
    
    -- Summary metrics
    pm_count_delta INTEGER,
    total_hours_delta DECIMAL(10,2),
    total_cost_delta DECIMAL(12,2),
    overloaded_weeks_delta INTEGER,
    
    -- Detailed differences (JSON)
    pm_differences JSONB,
    manhour_differences JSONB,
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);
```

## 3.10 Costing Tables

```sql
-- Labor rates
CREATE TABLE labor_rates (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),  -- NULL = global default
    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
    effective_to DATE,
    
    -- Rate types
    regular_hourly_rate DECIMAL(10,2) NOT NULL,
    overtime_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 1.5,
    weekend_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 2.0,
    holiday_multiplier DECIMAL(3,2) DEFAULT 2.5,
    
    -- Shop rate (fully loaded)
    shop_rate_hourly DECIMAL(10,2),  -- Includes overhead, benefits
    
    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(depot_id, effective_from)
);

-- Material cost history
CREATE TABLE material_costs (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
    effective_from DATE NOT NULL,
    effective_to DATE,
    
    unit_cost DECIMAL(12,2) NOT NULL,
    currency VARCHAR(3) DEFAULT 'SGD',
    supplier VARCHAR(100),
    
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(part_number, effective_from)
);

-- Cost baselines (for variance analysis)
CREATE TABLE cost_baselines (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
    fiscal_year INTEGER NOT NULL,
    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    
    budget_labor_hours DECIMAL(12,2),
    budget_labor_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
    budget_material_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
    budget_total DECIMAL(14,2),
    
    -- Actuals (updated monthly)
    actual_labor_hours DECIMAL(12,2),
    actual_labor_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
    actual_material_cost DECIMAL(14,2),
    actual_total DECIMAL(14,2),
    
    variance_pct DECIMAL(5,2) GENERATED ALWAYS AS 
        (CASE WHEN budget_total > 0 
              THEN ((actual_total - budget_total) / budget_total * 100)
              ELSE 0 END) STORED,
    
    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
    
    UNIQUE(fiscal_year, depot_id)
);
```

## 3.13 Canvas-Optimized Views

```sql
-- Strategic zoom level (yearly aggregates)
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_strategic AS
SELECT 
    t.id as train_id,
    t.train_number,
    t.fleet_id,
    date_trunc('year', ps.target_date) as year,
    COUNT(*) as pm_count,
    SUM(pt.estimated_hours) as total_hours,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ps.status = 'COMPLETED') as completed_count,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE ps.is_locked) as locked_count,
    SUM(pt.estimated_hours) FILTER (WHERE ps.status = 'SCHEDULED') as pending_hours
FROM trains t
LEFT JOIN pm_schedule ps ON t.id = ps.train_id AND ps.scenario_id IS NULL
LEFT JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
WHERE t.active = TRUE
GROUP BY t.id, t.train_number, t.fleet_id, date_trunc('year', ps.target_date);

CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_strategic_train ON v_canvas_strategic(train_id, year);

-- Tactical zoom level (weekly aggregates with PM details)
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW v_canvas_tactical AS
SELECT 
    t.id as train_id,
    t.train_number,
    date_trunc('week', ps.target_date) as week_start,
    ps.id as pm_id,
    ps.target_date,
    ps.status,
    ps.constraint_start,
    ps.constraint_end,
    ps.is_locked,
    pt.pm_code,
    pt.estimated_hours,
    pt.display_color,
    tr.track_number,
    d.code as depot_code
FROM trains t
JOIN pm_schedule ps ON t.id = ps.train_id AND ps.scenario_id IS NULL
JOIN pm_types pt ON ps.pm_type_id = pt.id
LEFT JOIN tracks tr ON ps.assigned_track_id = tr.id
LEFT JOIN depots d ON ps.assigned_depot_id = d.id
WHERE t.active = TRUE
  AND ps.status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS');

CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_tactical_train ON v_canvas_tactical(train_id, week_start);
CREATE INDEX idx_v_canvas_tactical_date ON v_canvas_tactical(target_date);

-- Man-hour canvas overlay
CREATE VIEW v_manhour_canvas AS
SELECT 
    mp.depot_id,
    d.code as depot_code,
    mp.period_start,
    mp.period_end,
    mp.required_total_hours,
    mp.available_total_hours,
    mp.variance_hours,
    mp.utilization_pct,
    mp.is_overloaded,
    mp.is_underutilized,
    COALESCE(adj.adjustment_count, 0) as pending_adjustments,
    COALESCE(adj.total_adjustment_hours, 0) as proposed_adjustment_hours
FROM manhour_projections mp
JOIN depots d ON mp.depot_id = d.id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT 
        projection_id,
        COUNT(*) as adjustment_count,
        SUM(hours_adjusted) as total_adjustment_hours
    FROM manhour_adjustments
    WHERE status = 'PROPOSED'
    GROUP BY projection_id
) adj ON mp.id = adj.projection_id
WHERE mp.projection_run_id = (
    SELECT projection_run_id 
    FROM manhour_projections 
    ORDER BY created_at DESC 
    LIMIT 1
);

-- Drift overlay (comparison to baseline)
CREATE VIEW v_drift_canvas AS
SELECT 
    da.train_id,
    t.train_number,
    da.baseline_id,
    pb.name as baseline_name,
    da.analysis_date,
    da.mileage_drift_km,
    da.mileage_drift_pct,
    da.avg_pm_drift_days,
    da.requires_replan,
    da.replan_reason,
    -- Color coding for canvas
    CASE 
        WHEN da.requires_replan THEN 'RED'
        WHEN ABS(da.mileage_drift_pct) > 3 THEN 'YELLOW'
        ELSE 'GREEN'
    END as drift_severity
FROM drift_analysis da
JOIN trains t ON da.train_id = t.id
JOIN plan_baselines pb ON da.baseline_id = pb.id
WHERE pb.status = 'PUBLISHED'
  AND da.analysis_date = (
      SELECT MAX(analysis_date) 
      FROM drift_analysis 
      WHERE baseline_id = da.baseline_id
  );

-- Refresh materialized views (scheduled job)
-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
-- REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
```

---

## 3.14 Database Maintenance

```sql
-- Automatic refresh of materialized views
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION refresh_canvas_views()
RETURNS void AS $$
BEGIN
    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_strategic;
    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY v_canvas_tactical;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Schedule refresh every 15 minutes (use pg_cron or external scheduler)
-- SELECT cron.schedule('*/15 * * * *', 'SELECT refresh_canvas_views()');

-- Cleanup expired scenarios
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION cleanup_expired_scenarios()
RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
DECLARE
    deleted_count INTEGER;
BEGIN
    UPDATE scenarios 
    SET status = 'EXPIRED'
    WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' 
      AND expires_at < NOW();
    
    GET DIAGNOSTICS deleted_count = ROW_COUNT;
    RETURN deleted_count;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Release expired soft reservations
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION release_expired_reservations()
RETURNS INTEGER AS $$
DECLARE
    released_count INTEGER;
BEGIN
    -- Update inventory positions
    UPDATE inventory_positions ip
    SET quantity_reserved = quantity_reserved - expired.total_qty
    FROM (
        SELECT inventory_position_id, SUM(quantity_reserved) as total_qty
        FROM material_reservations
        WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
          AND expires_at < NOW()
          AND fulfilled_at IS NULL
        GROUP BY inventory_position_id
    ) expired
    WHERE ip.id = expired.inventory_position_id;
    
    -- Delete expired reservations
    DELETE FROM material_reservations
    WHERE reservation_type = 'SOFT'
      AND expires_at < NOW()
      AND fulfilled_at IS NULL;
    
    GET DIAGNOSTICS released_count = ROW_COUNT;
    RETURN released_count;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;

-- Update train target mileage for fleet balancing
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION update_fleet_mileage_targets()
RETURNS void AS $$
BEGIN
    UPDATE trains t
    SET target_mileage_km = fleet_avg.avg_mileage
    FROM (
        SELECT fleet_id, AVG(current_mileage_km)::INTEGER as avg_mileage
        FROM trains
        WHERE active = TRUE
        GROUP BY fleet_id
    ) fleet_avg
    WHERE t.fleet_id = fleet_avg.fleet_id
      AND t.active = TRUE;
END;
$$ LANGUAGE plpgsql;
```

   223→
   224→### 6.3.5 Hybrid Rendering Architecture
   225→
   226→**CRITICAL:** The canvas uses **hybrid rendering** to optimize for each zoom level:
   227→
   228→| Zoom Level | Renderer | Rationale |
   229→|------------|----------|-----------|
   230→| Strategic (0-4) | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Heatmap cells, no text needed |
   231→| Tactical-Wide (4-8) | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Compressed blocks, minimal text |
   232→| Tactical (8-12) | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas)** | Gantt bars with crisp text labels |
   233→| Operational (12-16) | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas)** | Detailed cards with readable text |
   234→| Detail (16-20) | **React DOM** | Rich interactions, form inputs |
   235→
   236→**Why Hybrid?**
   237→
   238→deck.gl uses WebGL for rendering, which has limitations for text-heavy views:
   239→- Font atlases blur during zoom operations
   240→- Hit-testing for drag-and-drop is complex in WebGL
   241→- Text rendering feels less crisp than native canvas/DOM
   242→
   243→By switching to Konva (HTML5 Canvas 2D) at tactical zoom levels, we get:
   244→- Crisp text rendering at all scales
   245→- Native canvas hit-testing for smooth drag-and-drop
   246→- Better performance for hundreds of labeled PM blocks
   247→
   248→```typescript
   249→interface CanvasLayers {
   250→  // === DECK.GL LAYERS (Strategic/Overview) ===
   251→  // Used for zoom levels 0-8 where text is minimal
   252→  
   253→  strategicHeatmapLayer: HeatmapLayer;    // PM density heatmap
   254→  timelineAxisLayer: LineLayer;            // Time axis lines
   255→  quarterBoundaryLayer: LineLayer;         // Quarter separators
   256→  
   257→  // === KONVA LAYERS (Tactical/Operational) ===
   258→  // Used for zoom levels 8-16 where text labels are critical
   259→  
   260→  ganttBlockLayer: Konva.Layer;            // PM blocks with labels
   261→  constraintWallLayer: Konva.Layer;        // Compliance windows
   262→  trainLabelLayer: Konva.Layer;            // Train number labels
   263→  tooltipLayer: Konva.Layer;               // Hover tooltips
   264→  dragPreviewLayer: Konva.Layer;           // Drag-and-drop preview
   265→  
   266→  // === REACT DOM OVERLAY (Detail) ===
   267→  // Used for zoom level 16+ where rich interactions needed
   268→  
   269→  detailCardOverlay: React.ReactNode;      // Expanded PM cards
   270→  editFormOverlay: React.ReactNode;        // Inline editing
   271→}
   272→
   273→// Layer visibility by zoom level
   274→function getActiveRenderer(zoom: number): 'deckgl' | 'konva' | 'react' {
   275→  if (zoom < 8) return 'deckgl';
   276→  if (zoom < 16) return 'konva';
   277→  return 'react';
   278→}
   279→```
   280→
   281→**Transition Handling:**
   282→
   283→When crossing renderer boundaries, animate opacity to avoid jarring switches:
   284→
   285→```typescript
   286→function handleZoomChange(prevZoom: number, newZoom: number): void {
   287→  const prevRenderer = getActiveRenderer(prevZoom);
   288→  const newRenderer = getActiveRenderer(newZoom);
   289→  
   290→  if (prevRenderer !== newRenderer) {
   291→    // Cross-fade over 200ms
   292→    animateOpacity(prevRenderer, 1, 0, 200);
   293→    animateOpacity(newRenderer, 0, 1, 200);
   294→  }
   295→}
   296→```
   297→

   340→
   341→## 6.4 Drag-and-Drop Interaction
   342→
   343→### 6.4.1 PM Rescheduling Flow
   344→
   345→```
   346→User Action                     System Response
   347→───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   348→1. Hover over PM block         → Cursor changes to grab
   349→                               → Block highlights
   350→                               → Tooltip shows PM details
   351→
   352→2. Mouse down on PM block      → Block enters "dragging" state
   353→                               → Constraint walls become prominent
   354→                               → Other PMs fade slightly
   355→
   356→3. Drag PM to new position     → Ghost preview follows cursor
   357→                               → Real-time validation:
   358→                                 - Green outline = valid drop
   359→                                 - Red outline = constraint violation
   360→                               → Violation tooltip explains issue
   361→
   362→4. Release over valid position → Confirmation dialog:
   363→                                 "Move C2 for Train 1001 from
   364→                                  Feb 15 to Feb 20?"
   365→                                 [Cancel] [Confirm]
   366→
   367→5. User confirms               → API call to update PM
   368→                               → WebSocket broadcasts change
   369→                               → All connected clients update
   370→                               → History entry created
   371→                               → Success toast notification
   372→
   373→6. Release over invalid        → PM snaps back to original
   374→   position                    → Error toast explains violation
   375→```
   376→

   562→
   563→### 6.6.2 PM Scheduling Explanation

   604→
   605→## 6.7 Scenario Comparison View
   606→
   607→### 6.7.1 Side-by-Side Mode

   634→
   635→---
   636→
   637→## 6.8 Notification System
   638→
   639→### 6.8.1 Toast Notifications
   640→
   641→```typescript
   642→type ToastType = 'success' | 'warning' | 'error' | 'info';
   643→
   644→interface Toast {
   645→  id: string;
   646→  type: ToastType;
   647→  title: string;
   648→  message?: string;
   649→  action?: {
   650→    label: string;
   651→    onClick: () => void;
   652→  };
   653→  duration: number;  // ms, 0 = persistent
   654→}
   655→
   656→// Examples
   657→const TOAST_EXAMPLES = {
   658→  pmMoved: {
   659→    type: 'success',
   660→    title: 'PM Rescheduled',

   662→    duration: 5000
   663→  },
   664→  optimizerComplete: {

   666→    title: 'Optimization Complete',
   667→    message: '45 trains assigned, 3 unassigned',
   668→    action: { label: 'View Results', onClick: () => {} },
   669→    duration: 0  // Persistent until dismissed
   670→  },
   671→  constraintViolation: {
   672→    type: 'error',
   673→    title: 'Cannot Move PM',
   674→    message: 'Track T1 is occupied by another PM on this date',
   675→    duration: 8000
   676→  },
   677→  driftAlert: {
   678→    type: 'warning',
   679→    title: 'Drift Alert',
   680→    message: '3 trains require replanning attention',
   681→    action: { label: 'View Details', onClick: () => {} },

   683→  }

   686→
   687→### 6.8.2 Alert Banner
   688→
   689→For system-wide alerts:
   690→
   691→```
   692→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   693→│ ⚠️ BASELINE DRIFT ALERT: 3 trains have exceeded drift threshold.        │
   694→│    Review recommended before EOD.                          [View] [✕]  │
   695→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   696→```
   697→
   698→---
   699→
   700→## 6.9 Accessibility Requirements
   701→
   702→### 6.9.1 WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance
   703→
   704→| Requirement | Implementation |
   705→|-------------|----------------|
   706→| Keyboard navigation | Full canvas navigation via arrow keys, Tab for controls |
   707→| Screen reader | ARIA labels for all interactive elements |
   708→| Color contrast | 4.5:1 minimum for text, 3:1 for large text |
   709→| Focus indicators | Visible focus ring on all interactive elements |
   710→| Alternative text | All icons have text alternatives |
   711→| Reduced motion | Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` setting |
   712→
   713→### 6.9.2 Keyboard Shortcuts
   714→
   715→| Shortcut | Action |
   716→|----------|--------|
   717→| `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` | Navigate between panels |
   718→| `Arrow keys` | Pan canvas |
   719→| `+` / `-` | Zoom in/out |
   720→| `Home` | Go to today |
   721→| `1-5` | Switch zoom levels |
   722→| `Escape` | Cancel drag / Close dialog |
   723→| `Enter` | Activate selected element |
   724→| `Space` | Toggle selection |
   725→| `?` | Show keyboard shortcuts |
   726→
   727→---
   728→
   729→## 6.10 Responsive Behavior
   730→
   731→### 6.10.1 Breakpoints
   732→
   733→| Breakpoint | Width | Layout Changes |
   734→|------------|-------|----------------|
   735→| Desktop XL | ≥1920px | Full layout with all panels |
   736→| Desktop | ≥1440px | Standard layout |
   737→| Desktop SM | ≥1280px | Collapsible side panels |
   738→| Tablet | ≥1024px | Stacked panels, simplified canvas |
   739→| Mobile | <1024px | Not supported (show message) |
   740→
   741→### 6.10.2 Mobile Fallback
   742→
   743→```
   744→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   745→│                                                                 │
   746→│                    📱 Desktop Required                          │
   747→│                                                                 │
   748→│     The Metro Fleet Management System requires a desktop        │
   749→│     browser with a minimum resolution of 1024x768.              │
   750→│                                                                 │
   751→│     For the best experience, please use:                        │
   752→│     • Chrome 90+                                                │
   753→│     • Firefox 88+                                               │
   754→│     • Safari 14+                                                │
   755→│     • Edge 90+                                                  │
   756→│                                                                 │
   757→│     [View Read-Only Dashboard] (limited functionality)          │
   758→│                                                                 │
   759→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
   760→```
   761→
   762→---
   763→
   764→## 6.11 Performance Requirements
   765→
   766→| Metric | Target | Measurement |
   767→|--------|--------|-------------|
   768→| Initial load | <3s | Time to first meaningful paint |
   769→| Canvas render | 60 FPS | Frame rate during pan/zoom |
   770→| Data points | 500,000+ | Maximum renderable PM blocks |
   771→| Interaction response | <100ms | Time from click to visual feedback |
   772→| API response display | <500ms | Time from API response to UI update |
   773→| Memory usage | <1GB | Maximum heap size |
   774→
   775→### 6.11.1 Optimization Strategies
   776→
   777→```typescript
   778→const PERFORMANCE_OPTIMIZATIONS = {
   779→  // Data loading
   780→  virtualScrolling: true,           // Only render visible train rows
   781→  tileBasedLoading: true,           // Load canvas data in tiles
   782→  progressiveLoading: true,         // Load coarse data first, refine
   783→  
   784→  // Rendering
   785→  webWorkerCalculations: true,      // Offload heavy math to workers
   786→  gpuAcceleration: true,            // Use WebGL via deck.gl
   787→  memoizedFiltering: true,          // Cache filtered datasets
   788→  levelOfDetail: true,              // Reduce detail at low zoom
   789→  
   790→  // Caching
   791→  apiResponseCache: '5m',           // Cache API responses
   792→  canvasDataCache: '1m',            // Cache canvas tile data
   793→  userPreferencesCache: 'session',  // Cache user settings
   794→};
   795→```
   796→

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 6: User Interface Specification

**Document Version:** 2.0  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT

---

## 6.1 Design System Overview

### 6.1.1 Technology Stack

| Component | Technology | Purpose |
|-----------|------------|---------|
| Framework | React 18 | Component architecture |
| State | Zustand | Global state management |
| Canvas | deck.gl | WebGL timeline rendering |
| Components | BlueprintJS v6 | UI component library |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS | Utility-first styling |
| Build | Vite | Development and bundling |
| Types | TypeScript | Type safety |

### 6.1.2 Design Principles

1. **Information Density**: Maximize data visibility without overwhelming
2. **Semantic Zoom**: Data representation adapts to zoom level
3. **Direct Manipulation**: Drag-and-drop with immediate feedback
4. **Progressive Disclosure**: Details on demand
5. **Constraint Visibility**: Show limits, not just violations
6. **Explainability**: Every system decision has visible rationale

### 6.1.3 Color System

```typescript
const colors = {
  // PM Status
  pm: {
    scheduled: '#3B82F6',    // Blue
    inProgress: '#F59E0B',   // Amber
    completed: '#10B981',    // Green
    cancelled: '#6B7280',    // Gray
    deferred: '#8B5CF6',     // Purple
    overdue: '#EF4444',      // Red
  },
  
  // Constraint Indicators
  constraints: {
    hardViolation: '#DC2626',    // Red-600
    softViolation: '#F59E0B',    // Amber-500
    approaching: '#FBBF24',      // Yellow-400
    ok: '#10B981',               // Green-500
  },
  
  // Drift Severity
  drift: {
    critical: '#DC2626',     // Red
    warning: '#F59E0B',      // Amber
    ok: '#10B981',           // Green
  },
  
  // Utilization
  utilization: {
    overloaded: '#DC2626',   // Red
    optimal: '#10B981',      // Green
    underutilized: '#3B82F6', // Blue
  },
  
  // Fleet Colors
  fleets: {
    KS: '#0EA5E9',           // Sky-500
    ALSTOM: '#8B5CF6',       // Violet-500
  }
};
```

---

## 6.2 Application Layout

```

├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤

│ │ 245,000km │ │ │                                                             │ │

---

## 6.3 Infinite Canvas Component

### 6.3.1 Zoom Levels and Data Representation

| Zoom Level | Time Span | Granularity | Visual Representation |
|------------|-----------|-------------|----------------------|
| Strategic | 12 years | Yearly | Heatmap cells (PM density) |
| Tactical-Wide | 1 year | Monthly | Compressed Gantt blocks |
| Tactical | 12 weeks | Weekly | Full Gantt blocks with labels |
| Operational | 4 weeks | Daily | Detailed cards with all info |
| Detail | 1 week | Daily | Expanded cards with crew assignment |

### 6.3.2 Strategic View (Heatmap)

```
         │ 2026 │ 2027 │ 2028 │ 2029 │ 2030 │ 2031 │ ... │ 2035 │
─────────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼─────┼──────┤
Train    │      │      │      │      │      │      │     │      │
1001 KS  │ ████ │ ██░░ │ ████ │ ██░░ │ ████ │ ██░░ │ ... │ ████ │
─────────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼─────┼──────┤
1002 KS  │ ██░░ │ ████ │ ██░░ │ ████ │ ██░░ │ ████ │ ... │ ██░░ │
─────────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼─────┼──────┤
1003 KS  │ ████ │ ████ │ ██░░ │ ████ │ ████ │ ██░░ │ ... │ ████ │
─────────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼──────┼─────┼──────┤

Legend: ████ = High PM density (4+ PMs)
        ██░░ = Medium density (2-3 PMs)
        ░░░░ = Low density (0-1 PMs)
        
Hover: Shows PM count, total hours, cost estimate
Click: Zoom to that year
```

### 6.3.3 Tactical View (Gantt)

```
         │ Jan W1 │ Jan W2 │ Jan W3 │ Jan W4 │ Feb W1 │ Feb W2 │ Feb W3 │ Feb W4 │
─────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
1001 KS  │        │        │        │        │ ┌─────────────┐ │        │        │
         │        │        │        │        │ │ C2 - 24h    │ │        │        │
         │        │        │        │        │ │ Track: T1   │ │        │        │
         │        │        │        │        │ └─────────────┘ │        │        │
─────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
1002 KS  │ ┌────────────┐  │        │        │        │        │        │        │
         │ │ C1 - 8h    │  │        │        │        │        │        │        │
         │ └────────────┘  │        │        │        │        │        │        │
─────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤
1003 KS  │        │        │        │ ┌──────────────────────────────┐  │        │
         │        │        │        │ │ C3 - 72h                     │  │        │
         │        │        │        │ │ Track: T3 (Heavy) 🔒         │  │        │
         │        │        │        │ └──────────────────────────────┘  │        │
─────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┼────────┤

Constraint walls shown as vertical lines:
  ┃ = Compliance window start (earliest allowed)
  ┃ = Compliance window end (latest allowed - red if close)
  
Block colors indicate status (see color system)
🔒 = Locked PM (cannot be moved by optimizer)
```

### 6.3.4 Operational View (Detail Cards)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Train 1001 - Kinki Sharyo                                       │
│ C2 Inspection                                                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                 │
│  Date: Feb 15-17, 2026                                         │
│  Status: SCHEDULED                                              │
│  Track: T1 (Heavy Bay)                                          │
│  Depot: Bishan                                                  │
│                                                                 │
│  Hours: 24h estimated                                           │
│  Shift: Day shift                                               │
│                                                                 │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Compliance Window                                        │   │
│  │ ░░░░░░░░░░████████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ │   │
│  │ Feb 1          Feb 15        Feb 28                     │   │
│  │                  ▲                                       │   │
│  │            Scheduled                                     │   │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                 │

│  ✓ Brake Pad Set (8 available)                                 │
│  ✓ Filter Kit (12 available)                                   │
│  ⚠ Bogie Seal Kit (2 available, 4 needed - arriving Jan 20)   │
│                                                                 │
